creator: jeff+freebsd@wagsky.com
text:
There's definitely something here, as I'm suddenly getting this locally.

Two local resolvers, running unbound with stubby to upstream. One happens to be down right now, the other is in a jail on this host, if that gives someone a clue. 

The only recent change is that the .101 nameserver is not up right now.

RESOLVCONF is not set in the environment.

[jeff@js-apu ~]$ freebsd-version -ku
12.3-RELEASE-p5
12.3-RELEASE-p5

[jeff@js-apu ~]$ cat /etc/resolv.conf
nameserver	10.2.53.102
nameserver	10.2.53.101

[jeff@js-apu ~]$ drill pkg.freebsd.org
;; ->>HEADER<<- opcode: QUERY, rcode: NOERROR, id: 52182
;; flags: qr rd ra ; QUERY: 1, ANSWER: 3, AUTHORITY: 0, ADDITIONAL: 0 
;; QUESTION SECTION:
;; pkg.freebsd.org.	IN	A

;; ANSWER SECTION:
pkg.freebsd.org.	300	IN	CNAME	pkgmir.geo.freebsd.org.
pkgmir.geo.freebsd.org.	150	IN	A	213.138.116.73
pkgmir.geo.freebsd.org.	150	IN	A	139.178.72.201

;; AUTHORITY SECTION:

;; ADDITIONAL SECTION:

;; Query time: 348 msec
;; SERVER: 10.2.53.102
;; WHEN: Mon Apr 25 17:44:14 2022
;; MSG SIZE  rcvd: 90

[jeff@js-apu ~]$ ping pkg.freebsd.org
PING pkgmir.geo.freebsd.org (213.138.116.73): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 213.138.116.73: icmp_seq=0 ttl=35 time=146.526 ms
64 bytes from 213.138.116.73: icmp_seq=1 ttl=35 time=145.437 ms
^C
--- pkgmir.geo.freebsd.org ping statistics ---
2 packets transmitted, 2 packets received, 0.0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max/stddev = 145.437/145.982/146.526/0.544 ms


[jeff@js-apu ~]$ cat /etc/pkg/FreeBSD.conf
# $FreeBSD: releng/12.3/usr.sbin/pkg/FreeBSD.conf.quarterly 346780 2019-04-27 04:00:50Z cperciva $
#
# To disable this repository, instead of modifying or removing this file,
# create a /usr/local/etc/pkg/repos/FreeBSD.conf file:
#
#   mkdir -p /usr/local/etc/pkg/repos
#   echo "FreeBSD: { enabled: no }" > /usr/local/etc/pkg/repos/FreeBSD.conf
#

FreeBSD: {
#  url: "pkg+http://pkg.FreeBSD.org/${ABI}/quarterly",
  url: "pkg+http://pkg.FreeBSD.org/${ABI}/latest",
  mirror_type: "srv",
  signature_type: "fingerprints",
  fingerprints: "/usr/share/keys/pkg",
  enabled: yes
}


[jeff@js-apu ~]$ cat /usr/local/etc/pkg/repos/FreeBSD.conf 
# $FreeBSD: releng/12.1/usr.sbin/pkg/FreeBSD.conf.quarterly 346780 2019-04-27 04:00:50Z cperciva $
#
# To disable this repository, instead of modifying or removing this file,
# create a /usr/local/etc/pkg/repos/FreeBSD.conf file:
#
#   mkdir -p /usr/local/etc/pkg/repos
#   echo "FreeBSD: { enabled: no }" > /usr/local/etc/pkg/repos/FreeBSD.conf
#

FreeBSD: {
  url: "pkg+http://pkg.FreeBSD.org/${ABI}/latest",
  mirror_type: "srv",
  signature_type: "fingerprints",
  fingerprints: "/usr/share/keys/pkg",
  enabled: yes
}


[jeff@js-apu ~]$ sudo pkg update
Updating FreeBSD repository catalogue...
pkg: http://pkg.freebsd.org/FreeBSD:12:amd64/latest/meta.txz: No address record
repository FreeBSD has no meta file, using default settings
pkg: http://pkg.freebsd.org/FreeBSD:12:amd64/latest/packagesite.pkg: No address record
pkg: http://pkg.freebsd.org/FreeBSD:12:amd64/latest/packagesite.txz: No address record
Unable to update repository FreeBSD
Error updating repositories!


Bringing up the second nameserver "magically fixes" the problem

[jeff@js-apu ~]$ sudo pkg update
Password:
Updating FreeBSD repository catalogue...
Fetching meta.conf: 100%    163 B   0.2kB/s    00:01    
Fetching packagesite.pkg: 100%    6 MiB   1.3MB/s    00:05    
Processing entries: 100%
FreeBSD repository update completed. 31243 packages processed.
All repositories are up to date.


Taking the second nameserver down causes the problem to reappear.

Removing the second nameserver from /etc/resolv.conf (without a restart) does not "fix" the problem.



The running nameserver happens to be in a jail on this host (js-apu). It is ping-able

[jeff@js-apu ~]$ ping 10.2.53.102
PING 10.2.53.102 (10.2.53.102): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 10.2.53.102: icmp_seq=0 ttl=63 time=0.492 ms
64 bytes from 10.2.53.102: icmp_seq=1 ttl=63 time=0.363 ms
64 bytes from 10.2.53.102: icmp_seq=2 ttl=63 time=0.363 ms
^C
--- 10.2.53.102 ping statistics ---
3 packets transmitted, 3 packets received, 0.0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max/stddev = 0.363/0.406/0.492/0.061 ms
[
